name: Almond-Orange Biscotti
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Grease and flour baking sheet. Shake off excess.
3. In a large bowl, sift together flour, sugar, baking powder, and salt.
Make a well in the center of the mixture.
4. To the well, add eggs, vegetable oil, almond extract, zest of orange,
coarsely chopped almonds, and vegetable shortening (and walnuts or
pecans). Mix dry ingredients with liquids until a dough forms.
5. Divide the dough into two equal parts.
6. On a lightly floured surface, shape each part into flat-bottomed
cylinders that are 1 inch high, 2 1/2 inches wide, and 8 inches long.
Place each cylinder onto the baking sheet.
7. Bake for 30 to 35 minutes, until lightly colored on top. Remove from
oven and cool slightly.
8. Holding a sharp knife by the handle and tip, cut cylinders diagonally
into 3/4-inch slices. Carefully place the slices back onto the
baking sheet, with the cut sides down. Return to oven for an additional
15 minutes and bake until the sides are golden brown and the biscotti
have dried slightly.
9. Remove from oven and cool on wire racks. The biscotti should be hard
and crunchy. Yield: 20-24 biscotti. You can also dip one end into some
melted chocolate for a nice effect and great taste.
